# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.

Prevent hardcoded secrets.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php namespace App\Services; 
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Config;   // Import Config facade for accessing config files in Laravel environment variables or .env file, if not found then use array() function to get the value.
class SMSNotificationService {   
     private $twilio_accountSid; 
     private $twilio_authToken ;   // Declare your variable and assign them using Config::get('key') or you can also pass key as a string like new App\Services(Config::get('services.twilio')) if the configuration file is not in .env then use array() function to get value
     private $twilio_phoneNumber;  // Declare your variable and assign them using Config::get('key') or you can also pass key as a string like new App\Services(Config::get('services.twilio')) if the configuration file is not in .env then use array() function to get value
     private $client ;              // Twilio API client 
      public function __construct(){   /* Constructor */   
          $this->twilio_accountSid = Config::get('services.twilio.accountsid');// Access the configuration file and assign it to your variable using array() or get method if not found then use new App\Services(Config()) 
            // If you are passing key as a string like 'new SMSNotificationService("key")' in constructor, access that value from there.  
          $this->twilio_authToken = Config::get('services.twilio.authtoken');// Access the configuration file and assign it to your variable using array() or get method if not found then use new App\Services(Config()) 
          // If you are passing key as a string like 'new SMSNotificationService("key")' in constructor, access that value from there.  
          $this->twilio_phoneNumber = Config::get('services.twilio.phonenumber');// Access the configuration file and assign it to your variable using array() or get method if not found then use new App\Services(Config()) 
          // If you are passing key as a string like 'new SMSNotificationService("key")' in constructor, access that value from there.  
          $this->client = new \Twilio\Rest\Client( $this->twilio_accountSid ,  $this->twilio_authToken );  // Initialize TwiML client with your account sid and auth token    }     /* End of Constructor */
      public function sendMessage ($to, $from = null, $body){   // Method to handle message sending. If no from number is provided then use default twilio phoneNumber 
           if(is_null($from)){  $from = $this->twilio_phoneNumber;}    /* Default TwiML Phone Number */    
            try {   $message = $this->client->messages->create( $to, [   // Send SMS using twilioclient 
                'from' => $from ,     
                'body' =>$body]);       } catch (\Twilio\Exceptions\ApiException $e)    {     /* Catching Api Exception */        echo "Error: ".  $e->getMessage();   }} // Print the error message  if any exception is caught.}};// End of class
